raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 779 provides greater opportunity by expanding the types of 'earn and learn' programs recognized by the California Workforce Innovation Opportunity Act.",
    "Employment social enterprises and worker cooperatives are innovative, evidence-based approaches that train employees while they earn a paycheck.",
    "Offering skills training and employment together is one of the best ways to enable successful returns to the workforce.",
    "Today, 80% of all employees of employment social enterprises are justice-involved and 75% are Black or Latinx.",
    "Homeboy Industries employs almost 600 former gang members or previously incarcerated individuals while they undergo an 18-month job training program.",
    "The Community Housing Partnership’s Solutions SF trains and employs formerly homeless individuals in front desk staffing jobs.",
    "The Center for Employment Opportunities employs about 3,000 formerly incarcerated Californians annually in 10 locations.",
    "Goodwill California employs and trains over 19,000 Californians."
  ]
}
```
